subject: ap-world-history
title: Imperialism & Colonialism
stimulus:
“White folk who want to help Africa must be prepared to work with Africans on the basis of
complete racial equality. If the United States...is really worried about Communism taking root
in Africa and wants to prevent such a disaster from taking place, [as a former communist] I can
offer an assurance against it.1 This assurance will not only prevent Communism, but endear the
people of the great United States forever to the Africans.
Instead of supporting the discredited system of colonialism by propping up the European
regimes with military and financial aid, let American statesmen make a bold gesture to the
African in the spirit of the anti-colonialist tradition of 1776. This gesture should take the form of
an aid package for Africa similar to the Marshall Plan.”2
Source: George Padmore, Afro-Caribbean intellectual, essay published while he lived in
London, 1956
1: Padmore spent much of the 1920s and 1930s as a supporter of communism but became disillusioned with
Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

parts.prompt:
Identify one reason United States officials were concerned about communism as noted in the
first paragraph of the passage.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.

required_elements: The Cold War struggle between pro- and anti-communist forces played out on a global scale, including the African continent.
alternatives.correct_example: The Cold War struggle between pro- and anti-communist forces played out on a global scale, including the African continent.

required_elements: United States officials were concerned that the Soviet Union would seek to spread its support for communism to the developing world.
alternatives.correct_example: United States officials were concerned that the Soviet Union would seek to spread its support for communism to the developing world.

required_elements: United States officials may have been concerned that if one African country were to fall under the influence of the communist Soviet government, more African countries may follow suit.
alternatives.correct_example: United States officials may have been concerned that if one African country were to fall under the influence of the communist Soviet government, more African countries may follow suit.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.

parts.prompt:
Describe the historical situation that resulted in the “system of colonialism” referred to by
the author in the second paragraph of the passage.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.

required_elements: Beginning in the fifteenth century, European states began establishing trading post empires on the coasts of Africa to obtain slaves and sought-after trade goods.
alternatives.correct_example: Beginning in the fifteenth century, European states began establishing trading post empires on the coasts of Africa to obtain slaves and sought-after trade goods.

required_elements: In the nineteenth century, Great Britain, France, and Germany, and, to a lesser extent Portugal, Spain, and Italy, carved out and took over most of the territory of the African continent in the “Scramble for Africa.”
alternatives.correct_example: In the nineteenth century, Great Britain, France, and Germany, and, to a lesser extent Portugal, Spain, and Italy, carved out and took over most of the territory of the African continent in the “Scramble for Africa.”

required_elements: Economic and military rivalries between European powers as well as resistance and/or accommodation from African rulers and states shaped the process of European colonization.
alternatives.correct_example: Economic and military rivalries between European powers as well as resistance and/or accommodation from African rulers and states shaped the process of European colonization.

required_elements: In a small number of African territories, European migration resulted in the establishment of settler colonies.
alternatives.correct_example: In a small number of African territories, European migration resulted in the establishment of settler colonies.

wrong_examples: A vague statement without specific historical evidence.
Restating the prompt without providing a defensible response.
Providing an example not relevant to the specific period or region asked.
common_traps: Identifies a person or event without explaining its historical relevance.
Provides multiple examples when only one is needed — focus on quality not quantity.

parts.prompt:
Explain one way the source reflects the political situation in Africa during the second half of
the twentieth century.

scoring_points.description: Earns 1 point for addressing the prompt with historically defensible content.

required_elements: The source’s reference to the impending threat of communism becoming established in Africa reflects the political situation in Africa during the Cold War as the United States and Soviet Union were jostling for influence.
alternatives.correct_example: The source’s reference to the impending threat of communism becoming established in Africa reflects the political situation in Africa during the Cold War as the United States and Soviet Union were jostling for influence.

required_elements: The author’s reference to the “discredited system of Colonialism” by Europeans shows the ongoing struggle of many African nations to liberate themselves from imperialism.
alternatives.correct_example: The author’s reference to the “discredited system of Colonialism” by Europeans shows the ongoing struggle of many African nations to liberate themselves from imperialism.

required_elements: The author’s reference to “an aid package for Africa similar to the Marshall Plan” associates the continent’s struggle to rebuild during decolonization with the rebuilding of Europe after the Second World War.
alternatives.correct_example: The author’s reference to “an aid package for Africa similar to the Marshall Plan” associates the continent’s struggle to rebuild during decolonization with the rebuilding of Europe after the Second World War.
